volumeTitle: Children of Heracles. Hippolytus. Andromache. Hecuba
label: Hippolytus (Section 1280 - Section 1431)

sections.label: Section 1280
sections.original: ἄνδρας τε · συμπάντων βασιληίδα τιμάν ,
sections.translation: and man ; thine , O Cypris , thine alone the sovereign power to rule them all .

sections.label: Section 1283
sections.original: σὲ τὸν εὐπατρίδην Αἰγέως κέλομαι
sections.translation: Hearken , I bid thee , noble son of Aegeus :

sections.label: Section 1285
sections.original: Λητοῦς δὲ κόρη σ ’ Ἄρτεμις αὐδῶ .
sections.translation: lo ! ’ tis I Latona ’ s child , that speak , I , Artemis . Why , Theseus , to thy sorrow dost thou rejoice at these tidings , seeing that thou hast slain thy son most impiously , listening to a charge not clearly proved , but falsely sworn to by thy wife ? though clearly has the curse therefrom upon thee fallen .

sections.label: Section 1290
sections.original: πῶς οὐχ ὑπὸ γῆς τάρταρα κρύπτεις
sections.translation: Why dost thou not for very shame hide beneath the dark places of the earth , or change thy human life and soar on wings to escape this tribulation ? ’ Mongst men of honour thou hast

sections.label: Section 1295
sections.original: κτητὸν βιότου μέρος ἐστίν .
sections.translation: now no share in life .

sections.label: Section 1296
sections.original: ἄκουε , Θησεῦ , σῶν κακῶν κατάστασιν ·
sections.translation: Hearken , Theseus ; I will put thy wretched case . Yet will it naught avail thee , if I do , but vex thy heart ; still with this intent I came , to show thy son ’ s pure heart ,— that he may die with honour ,—

sections.label: Section 1300
sections.original: καὶ σῆς γυναικὸς οἶστρον ἢ τρόπον τινὰ
sections.translation: as well the frenzy , and , in a sense , the nobleness of thy wife ; for she was cruelly stung with a passion for thy son by that goddess whom all we , that joy in virgin purity , detest . And though she strove to conquer love by resolution ,

sections.label: Section 1305
sections.original: τροφοῦ διώλετ ’ οὐχ ἑκοῦσα μηχαναῖς ,
sections.translation: yet by no fault of hers she fell , thanks to her nurse ’ s strategy , who did reveal her malady unto thy son under oath . But he would none of her counsels , as indeed was right , nor yet , when thou didst revile him , would he break the oath he swore , from piety .

sections.label: Section 1310
sections.original: ἣ δ ’ εἰς ἔλεγχον μὴ πέσῃ φοβουμένη
sections.translation: She meantime , fearful of being found out , wrote a lying letter , destroying by guile thy son , but yet persuading thee .

sections.label: Section 1313
sections.original: οἴμοι .
sections.translation: Woe is me !

sections.label: Section 1313a
sections.original: δάκνει σε , Θησεῦ , μῦθος ; ἀλλ ’ ἔχ ’ ἥσυχος ,
sections.translation: Doth my story wound thee , Theseus ? Be still awhile ; hear what follows , so wilt thou have more cause to groan .

sections.label: Section 1315
sections.original: ἆρ ’ οἶσθα πατρὸς τρεῖς ἀρὰς ἔχων σαφεῖς ;
sections.translation: Dost remember those three prayers thy father granted thee , fraught with certain issue ? ’ Tis one of these thou hast misused , unnatural wretch , against thy son , instead of aiming it at an enemy . Thy sea-god sire , ’ tis true , for all his kind intent , hath granted that boon he was compelled , by reason of his promise , to grant .

sections.label: Section 1320
sections.original: σὺ δ ’ ἔν τ ’ ἐκείνῳ κἀν ἐμοὶ φαίνῃ κακός ,
sections.translation: But thou alike in his eyes and in mine hast shewn thy evil heart , in that thou hast forestalled all proof or voice prophetic , hast made no inquiry , nor taken time for consideration , but with undue haste cursed thy son even to the death .

sections.label: Section 1325
sections.original: δέσποιν ’, ὀλοίμην .
sections.translation: Perdition seize me ! Queen revered !

sections.label: Section 1325b
sections.original: δείν ’ ἔπραξας , ἀλλ ’ ὅμως
sections.translation: An awful deed was thine , but still even for this thou mayest obtain pardon ; for it was Cypris that would have it so , sating the fury of her soul . For this is law amongst us gods ; none of us will thwart

sections.label: Section 1330
sections.original: τῇ τοῦ θέλοντος , ἀλλ ’ ἀφιστάμεσθ ’ ἀεί .
sections.translation: his neighbour ’ s will , but ever we stand aloof . For be well assured , did I not fear Zeus , never would I have incurred the bitter shame of handing over to death a man of all his kind to me most dear . As for thy sin ,

sections.label: Section 1335
sections.original: τὸ μὴ εἰδέναι μὲν πρῶτον ἐκλύει κάκης ·
sections.translation: first thy ignorance absolves thee from its villainy , next thy wife , who is dead , was lavish in her use of convincing arguments to influence thy mind .

sections.label: Section 1338
sections.original: μάλιστα μέν νυν σοὶ τάδ ’ ἔρρωγεν κακά ,
sections.translation: On thee in chief this storm of woe hath burst , yet is it some grief to me as well ; for when the righteous die , there is no joy in heaven ,

sections.label: Section 1340
sections.original: θνῄσκοντας οὐ χαίρουσι · τούς γε μὴν κακοὺς
sections.translation: albeit we try to destroy the wicked , house and home .

sections.label: Section 1342
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ὁ τάλας ὅδε δὴ στείχει ,
sections.translation: Lo ! where he comes , this hapless youth , his fair young flesh and auburn locks most shamefully handled . Unhappy house !

sections.label: Section 1345
sections.original: οἷον ἐκράνθη δίδυμον μελάθροις
sections.translation: what twofold sorrow doth o ’ ertake its halls , through heaven ’ s ordinance !

sections.label: Section 1347
sections.original: αἰαῖ αἰαῖ ·
sections.translation: Ah ! ah ! woe is me ! foully undone by an impious father ’ s impious imprecation !

sections.label: Section 1350
sections.original: ἀπόλωλα τάλας , οἴμοι μοι .
sections.translation: Undone , undone ! woe is me ! Through my head shoot fearful pains ; my brain throbs convulsively . Stop , let me rest my worn-out frame . Oh , oh !

sections.label: Section 1355
sections.original: ὦ στυγνὸν ὄχημ ’ ἵππειον , ἐμῆς
sections.translation: Accursed steeds , that mine own hand did feed , ye have been my ruin and my death . O by the gods , good sirs , I beseech ye , softly touch my wounded limbs .

sections.label: Section 1360
sections.original: τίς ἐφέστηκεν δεξιὰ πλευροῖς ;
sections.translation: Who stands there at my right side ? Lift me tenderly ; with slow and even step conduct a poor wretch cursed by his mistaken sire . Great Zeus , dost thou see this ? Me thy reverent worshipper ,

sections.label: Section 1365
sections.original: ὅδ ’ ὁ σωφροσύνῃ πάντας ὑπερσχὼν
sections.translation: me who left all men behind in purity , plunged thus into yawning Hades ’ neath the earth , reft of life ; in vain the toils I have endured through my piety towards mankind .

sections.label: Section 1370
sections.original: αἰαῖ αἰαῖ ·
sections.translation: Ah me ! ah me ! O the thrill of anguish shooting through me ! Set me down , poor wretch I am ; come Death to set me free ! Kill me , end my sufferings . [*]

sections.label: Section 1375
sections.original: μονά μ ’· ἀμφιτόμου λόγχας ἔραμαι ,
sections.translation: O for a sword two-edged to hack my flesh , and close this mortal life ! Ill-fated curse of my father ! the crimes of bloody kinsmen . [*] ancestors of old ,

sections.label: Section 1380
sections.original: προγεννητόρων ἐξορίζεται
sections.translation: now pass their boundaries and tarry not , and upon me are they come all guiltless as I am ; ah ! why ? Alas , alas !

sections.label: Section 1385
sections.original: — τί φῶ ; πῶς ἀπαλλάξω βιοτὰν ἐμὰν τάνδ ’ ἀναλ-
sections.translation: what can I say ? How from my life get rid of this relentless agony ? O that the stern Death-god , night ’ s black visitant , would give my sufferings rest !

sections.label: Section 1389
sections.original: ὦ τλῆμον , οἵᾳ συμφορᾷ συνεζύγης ·
sections.translation: Poor sufferer ! cruel the fate that links thee to it !

sections.label: Section 1390
sections.original: τὸ δ ’ εὐγενές σε τῶν φρενῶν ἀπώλεσεν .
sections.translation: Thy noble soul hath been thy ruin .

sections.label: Section 1391
sections.original: ἔα ·
sections.translation: Ah ! the fragrance from my goddess wafted ! Even in my agony I feel thee near and find relief ; she is here in this very place , my goddess Artemis .

sections.label: Section 1394
sections.original: ὦ τλῆμον , ἔστι , σοί γε φιλτάτη θεῶν .
sections.translation: She is , poor sufferer ! the goddess thou hast loved the best .

sections.label: Section 1395
sections.original: ὁρᾷς με , δέσποιν ’, ὡς ἔχω , τὸν ἄθλιον ;
sections.translation: Dost see me , mistress mine ? dost see my present suffering ?

sections.label: Section 1396
sections.original: ὁρῶ · κατ ’ ὄσσων δ ’ οὐ θέμις βαλεῖν δάκρυ .
sections.translation: I see thee , but mine eyes no tear may weep .

sections.label: Section 1397
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστι σοι κυναγὸς οὐδ ’ ὑπηρέτης ,
sections.translation: Thou hast none now to lead the hunt or tend thy fane .

sections.label: Section 1398
sections.original: οὐ δῆτ ’· ἀτάρ μοι προσφιλής γ ’ ἀπόλλυσαι .
sections.translation: None now ; yet e ’ en in death I love thee still .

sections.label: Section 1399
sections.original: οὐδ ’ ἱππονώμας οὐδ ’ ἀγαλμάτων φύλαξ .
sections.translation: None to groom thy steeds , or guard thy shrines .

sections.label: Section 1400
sections.original: Κύπρις γὰρ ἡ πανοῦργος ὧδ ’ ἐμήσατο .
sections.translation: Twas Cypris , mistress of iniquity , devised this evil .

sections.label: Section 1401
sections.original: ὤμοι · φρονῶ δὴ δαίμον ’ ἥ μ ’ ἀπώλεσεν .
sections.translation: Ah me ! now know I the goddess who destroyed me .

sections.label: Section 1402
sections.original: τιμῆς ἐμέμφθη σωφρονοῦντι δ ’ ἤχθετο .
sections.translation: She was jealous of her slighted honour , vexed at thy chaste life .

sections.label: Section 1403
sections.original: τρεῖς ὄντας ἡμᾶς ὤλεσ ’, ᾔσθημαι , Κύπρις .
sections.translation: Ah ! then I see her single hand hath struck down three of us .

sections.label: Section 1404
sections.original: πατέρα γε καὶ σὲ καὶ τρίτην ξυνάορον .
sections.translation: Thy sire and thee , and last thy father ’ s wife .

sections.label: Section 1405
sections.original: ᾤμωξα τοίνυν καὶ πατρὸς δυσπραξίας .
sections.translation: My sire ’ s ill-luck as well as mine I mourn .

sections.label: Section 1406
sections.original: ἐξηπατήθη δαίμονος βουλεύμασιν .
sections.translation: He was deceived by a goddess ’ s design .

sections.label: Section 1407
sections.original: ὦ δυστάλας σὺ τῆσδε συμφορᾶς , πάτερ .
sections.translation: Woe is thee , my father , in this sad mischance !

sections.label: Section 1408
sections.original: ὄλωλα , τέκνον , οὐδέ μοι χάρις βίου .
sections.translation: My son , I am a ruined man ; life has no joys for me .

sections.label: Section 1409
sections.original: στένω σὲ μᾶλλον ἢ ’ μὲ τῆς ἁμαρτίας .
sections.translation: For this mistake I mourn thee rather than myself .

sections.label: Section 1410
sections.original: εἰ γὰρ γενοίμην , τέκνον , ἀντὶ σοῦ νεκρός .
sections.translation: O that I had died for thee , my son !

sections.label: Section 1411
sections.original: ὦ δῶρα πατρὸς σοῦ Ποσειδῶνος πικρά .
sections.translation: Ah ! those fatal gifts thy sire Poseidon gave .

sections.label: Section 1412
sections.original: ὡς μήποτ ’ ἐλθεῖν ὤφελ ’ ἐς τοὐμὸν στόμα .
sections.translation: Would God these lips had never uttered that prayer !

sections.label: Section 1413
sections.original: τί δ ’; ἔκτανές τἄν μ ’, ὡς τότ ’ ἦσθ ’ ὠργισμένος .
sections.translation: Why not ? thou wouldst in any case have slain me in thy fury then .

sections.label: Section 1414
sections.original: δόξης γὰρ ἦμεν πρὸς θεῶν ἐσφαλμένοι .
sections.translation: Yes ; Heaven had perverted my power to think .

sections.label: Section 1415
sections.original: φεῦ ·
sections.translation: O that the race of men could bring a curse upon the gods !

sections.label: Section 1416
sections.original: ἔασον · οὐ γὰρ οὐδὲ γῆς ὑπὸ ζόφον
sections.translation: Enough ! for though thou pass to gloom beneath the earth , the wrath of Cypris shall not , at her will , fall on thee unrequited , because thou hadst a noble righteous soul . [*]

sections.label: Section 1420
sections.original: ἐγὼ γὰρ αὐτῆς ἄλλον ἐξ ἐμῆς χερὸς
sections.translation: For I with mine own hand will with these unerring shafts avenge me on another , [*] who is her votary , dearest to her of all the sons of men . And to thee , poor sufferer , for thy anguish now will I grant high honours in the city of Troezen ;

sections.label: Section 1425
sections.original: δώσω · κόραι γὰρ ἄζυγες γάμων πάρος
sections.translation: for thee shall maids unwed before their marriage cut off their hair , thy harvest through the long roll of time of countless bitter tears . Yea , and for ever shall the virgin choir hymn thy sad memory ,

sections.label: Section 1430
sections.original: ἔρως ὁ Φαίδρας ἐς σὲ σιγηθήσεται .
sections.translation: nor shall Phaedra ’ s love for thee fall into oblivion and pass away unnoticed .

sections.label: Section 1431
sections.original: σὺ δ ’, ὦ γεραιοῦ τέκνον Αἰγέως , λαβὲ
sections.translation: But thou , O son of old Aegeus , take thy son in thine arms , draw him close to thee , for unwittingly thou slewest him , and men may well commit an error when gods put it in their way .
